headline: Harbor Insurance is in active implementation crisis with a declining health score (48), a frustrated executive sponsor blocked by recurring SSO failures, and steady user drop-off — all 39 days ahead of a $240K renewal.
objectives: Confirm a concrete, time-bound SSO resolution path with committed engineering/support owners before the call ends
Present and align on a realistic rollout unblock plan with clear milestones the executive sponsor can hold onto
Stabilize executive confidence by demonstrating accountability, urgency, and a credible recovery narrative
topics_to_cover: SSO failure status: latest diagnosis from support on T001, root cause, and ETA to fix
Escalation ownership: confirm who internally owns the SSO resolution and provide direct contact to Harbor
Rollout unblock plan: phased milestones, revised go-live target, and what Harbor needs to do vs. what we own
Active user decline: acknowledge the trend (301 → 245 over two weeks) and tie recovery plan to reversing it
Executive sponsor confidence: address frustration directly, set realistic expectations, and establish a communication cadence
Renewal timeline awareness: renewal is 2026-06-12 — recovery must show visible progress within 2–3 weeks
open_followups_from_last_call: CSM (Morgan Lee) committed on 2026-04-17 to coordinate a technical escalation for the SSO blocker — confirm this was actioned and share outcome with the customer
risks_to_flag: Renewal at risk: 2026-06-12 renewal is 39 days away with health score at 48 and NPS of 2 — insufficient time to recover if SSO is not resolved within the next 7–10 days
User attrition is accelerating: active users down ~19% in two weeks; if rollout remains blocked, disengagement may become structural and hard to reverse
Executive sponsor frustration (T001): a dissatisfied exec sponsor this close to renewal significantly elevates churn probability — any perceived lack of urgency on this call could be a tipping point
No buffer for further delays: with 9 support tickets in 30 days and declining power-user engagement, the account has low tolerance for additional technical setbacks
questions_to_ask_customer: Since the SSO failures began, which specific teams or workflows have been most disrupted, and how is that affecting your team's confidence in the rollout?
What would a credible recovery plan need to include — in terms of timeline, communication, and visible milestones — for your executive sponsor to feel the situation is under control?
Beyond the SSO issue, are there any other blockers or concerns on your side that we haven't addressed yet that could slow the rollout once SSO is resolved?
Given where things stand today, what does a successful outcome look like for Harbor Insurance by the end of May?

headline: Northstar Travel enters a high-stakes call 15 days before renewal with a declining health score (52), a broken mobile onboarding flow blocking field-team adoption, and an NPS of 3—making resolution of T003 and renewal confidence the critical priorities.
objectives: Confirm status and expected resolution timeline of T003 (mobile onboarding bug) and assess its direct impact on the renewal decision
Align on a concrete mobile adoption recovery plan to arrest the declining active-user trend before the May 20 renewal
Secure a clear renewal intent signal and identify any outstanding stakeholder concerns or blockers
topics_to_cover: Status update on open ticket T003: mobile onboarding bug affecting field users
Impact of the onboarding issue on active user count (99 → 87 over 3 weeks) and power-user drop
Delivery of the mobile onboarding plan promised on 2026-04-16 (overdue follow-up)
Renewal timeline discussion: May 20 deadline, decision-maker alignment, and any commercial concerns
Short-term adoption recovery steps the CSM or product team can support before renewal date
Customer sentiment and NPS score of 3—acknowledge and address dissatisfaction directly
open_followups_from_last_call: Send mobile onboarding plan to Northstar Travel team (committed 2026-04-16, not yet confirmed delivered)
risks_to_flag: Renewal is 15 days out with a health score of 52 and NPS of 3—churn risk is high if T003 is not resolved or credibly committed to
T003 (mobile onboarding bug) is still open as of 2026-04-28 with negative sentiment; unresolved bugs at renewal are a common churn trigger
Active users have declined 12% in three weeks (99 → 87); if this trend continues through renewal, it weakens the ROI case for the customer
Power-user count dropped significantly (37 → 30) suggesting even engaged users are disengaging, not just casual ones
Gap of 19 days since last contact (2026-04-16) with no confirmed follow-up delivery is a relationship continuity risk
questions_to_ask_customer: Since the mobile onboarding issue was flagged, how has it affected your field team's day-to-day workflow, and have any team members stopped trying to use the app as a result?
As you approach the renewal decision, what would need to be true—either resolved or credibly committed to—for you to feel confident moving forward with us?
Beyond the onboarding bug, are there other pain points or unmet expectations that are influencing how your team feels about the platform right now?
Who else on your side is involved in the renewal conversation, and is there anything I should be aware of going into that discussion?

headline: Cobalt Health is on a strong growth trajectory with weekly active users up 21% over the past two weeks and high expansion appetite from the analytics team, entering a call focused on new department rollout planning.
objectives: Align on a concrete rollout plan and timeline for the analytics department expansion
Deliver or walk through best-practice configuration guidance to unblock T012 and reduce setup risk
Identify a champion or project owner on the customer side to drive the rollout to completion
topics_to_cover: Status update on analytics team expansion interest since April 25 discussion
Best-practice setup recommendations for the new department (directly addressing T012)
Rollout plan review: timeline, stakeholders, seat estimates, and phased onboarding approach
Usage growth context — acknowledge 294 active / 164 power-users milestone as a positive signal
Any configuration or permission requirements specific to the analytics team's use case
Renewal timeline awareness (2026-12-01) and how expansion supports long-term value realization
open_followups_from_last_call: Share rollout plan with Cobalt Health (committed on 2026-04-25 — confirm this has been sent or deliver it on this call)
risks_to_flag: Best-practice configuration gap identified in T012 remains unresolved — delays here could slow adoption in the new department
No named internal project owner confirmed for the analytics rollout, which could stall execution momentum
Rapid user growth (243→294 actives in two weeks) may surface capacity or governance questions that should be proactively addressed before the new department goes live
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: With NPS of 9, a health score trending up to 88, and a clear internal champion in the analytics team, this is a strong moment to formalize the expansion scope and scope out incremental seats or a potential upsell tier. Morgan should also explore whether the account would be willing to participate in a case study or reference program given the compelling usage growth story.
questions_to_ask_customer: Who within the analytics team is leading the rollout effort, and what does their timeline look like for getting the new department onboarded?
What specific workflows or use cases is the analytics team most excited to solve — and are there any gaps in the current configuration that would block those?
Since we last spoke, have any other teams or stakeholders expressed interest in using the platform beyond the analytics group?
What would a successful rollout look like for you 90 days from now — how will you measure whether it's working?

headline: Futura Robotics is showing strong and accelerating adoption (98 active users, 54 power users) with high expansion signals around premium automation, pending internal budget approval.
objectives: Confirm whether the premium automation options sent post-last-call were reviewed and where budget approval stands
Walk through premium automation feature limits and relevant use cases to address T011 and advance the expansion conversation
Identify a concrete next step or timeline toward a premium upgrade before the 2026-09-30 renewal
topics_to_cover: Status of budget approval process for premium automation (carried from 2026-04-22 call)
Review of premium automation options shared after last call
Premium automation feature limits — scope, thresholds, and what unlocks at each tier
Specific use cases from Futura Robotics' workflow that map to premium capabilities
Usage growth trajectory (77 → 98 active users in 2 weeks) and what it means for their current plan limits
Renewal timeline awareness and any procurement lead time to keep in mind
open_followups_from_last_call: Send premium automation options (2026-04-22 action item) — confirm receipt and whether the team has reviewed the materials
Budget approval was flagged as a risk — follow up on approval status and who the internal decision-maker is
risks_to_flag: Budget approval has not been confirmed — if stalled, renewal expansion could be at risk with only ~5 months to go
Rapid user growth (27% increase in active users over 2 weeks) may cause the account to hit current plan limits before renewal, creating friction if premium is not adopted proactively
T011 is still unresolved (status: new) — leaving the automation limits question unanswered may slow the expansion decision
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Futura Robotics is a strong candidate for a premium automation upgrade given accelerating power-user growth, a high NPS of 8, and an inbound signal (T011) showing self-motivated curiosity about premium features. If the upgrade closes before renewal, there is a meaningful ACV increase opportunity. The account could also be positioned as a reference customer for mid-market robotics use cases given their strong adoption story.
questions_to_ask_customer: Since we sent over the premium automation options after our last call, has your team had a chance to review them — and what was the reaction from the stakeholders involved in the budget decision?
As your active user base has grown significantly over the past few weeks, are there specific automation workflows that are starting to feel constrained by your current plan limits?
What does the internal approval process look like for a potential upgrade, and is there a timeline or milestone we should be working toward together?
Which premium automation use case, based on what you've seen so far, feels most immediately valuable to your team's day-to-day operations?

headline: Juniper Manufacturing is entering a critical pre-renewal window with a 3-week consecutive decline in active and power users, unresolved data trust concerns around report exports, and a health score dropping to 58 — making this call pivotal to stabilizing the account before the July 2 renewal.
objectives: Resolve or create a clear action plan for the data freshness/export trust issue identified on the 4/24 call to restore confidence in the reporting module
Understand the root cause of department-level usage drop and align on concrete re-engagement steps with Juniper's internal champions
Assess renewal risk and gauge customer sentiment directly, given NPS of 5 and declining health score
topics_to_cover: Status update on data freshness investigation (carry-forward from 4/24 call)
Walk through T004: power user's inability to interpret dashboard drop — confirm whether the issue is UX, data accuracy, or underlying technical bug
Review active user decline trend (104 → 94 → 87 active users over 3 weeks) and identify which departments are most affected
Address export data trust concern — share any findings, workarounds, or fix timelines
Discuss impact on day-to-day workflows for affected departments
Outline a short-term recovery plan with owner and milestone dates
Light renewal framing: understand where Juniper's stakeholders stand heading into July
open_followups_from_last_call: Investigate data freshness issue flagged on 2026-04-24 call — resolution or findings must be shared on this call
risks_to_flag: Renewal is 56 days out (2026-07-02) with a declining health score (58) and NPS of 5 — churn risk is real if usage and trust issues are not resolved quickly
Power-user count has dropped from 38 to 27 in three weeks — loss of internal champions could accelerate disengagement and make renewal negotiation harder
Unresolved report export trust issue may be causing users to abandon the reporting module entirely, creating a compounding adoption problem
6 support tickets in 30 days signals product friction beyond a single issue — CSM should probe whether there is a broader satisfaction problem or a specific workflow breakdown
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Expansion signal is rated medium, but should not be pursued until health score stabilizes and data trust issues are resolved. If this call results in a clear fix timeline and visible progress, the recovery itself could be reframed as a proof point — positioning Juniper for a case study or reference once the account is back on track post-renewal.
questions_to_ask_customer: Since our last call, have your team's concerns about the accuracy of the exported reports changed at all — and is the reporting module still being actively used or have teams started working around it?
Which specific departments have pulled back on usage, and what are they telling you about why they've stepped away from the platform?
When you think about what 'good' looks like for the reporting module three months from now, what would need to be true for your power users to feel confident in the data again?
As we head toward your July renewal, what questions or concerns do you anticipate coming from your internal stakeholders when they evaluate whether to continue?

headline: Quartz Media enters this reset call in a critical at-risk state — usage has dropped 24% in three weeks, NPS is 2, no internal champion exists, and renewal is 76 days out.
objectives: Identify and confirm a named internal sponsor or champion who will own platform adoption going forward
Agree on a concrete reactivation plan with specific milestones and a 30-day check-in cadence
Assess whether the renewal at 2026-07-22 is viable or requires commercial restructuring
topics_to_cover: Acknowledge the team's disengagement openly and validate their frustration (NPS 2, negative ticket T006)
Champion gap: who is currently accountable for the platform internally, and can a new sponsor be appointed today
Root cause of usage decline: workflow fit, onboarding gaps, competing tools, or org change
Platform value re-alignment: revisit original use case and identify any quick wins still achievable
Reactivation plan: proposed structure, timeline, success criteria, and CSM support commitment
Open ticket T006: confirm what a 'reset' means to them and set shared expectations
Renewal timeline: transparent conversation about the 2026-07-22 date and what needs to be true to justify it
open_followups_from_last_call: Identify a new internal sponsor to replace the absent champion — unresolved from 2026-04-09 call
Propose and align on a formal platform reset plan — committed on 2026-04-09 but not yet delivered
risks_to_flag: No champion in place: without a named internal owner agreed on this call, any reactivation plan will stall
Usage trajectory is steep and worsening (21 → 16 active users, 7 → 3 power users in 14 days) — passive churn is already underway
76 days to renewal with NPS 2 and health score 46 is insufficient runway if reactivation does not start immediately
High-severity ticket T006 is open and unresolved — customer has explicitly stated the team stopped using the platform
28-day gap since last contact (2026-04-09 to 2026-05-07) has allowed negative sentiment to compound without intervention
questions_to_ask_customer: Since your team stepped back from the platform, what's changed internally — in your workflow, your team structure, or your priorities — that we'd need to account for in any reset plan?
Who in your organization would need to see clear value demonstrated over the next 30 days to feel confident about the renewal in July?
When you say you need a 'reset,' what does success look like to you specifically — what would the platform need to be doing differently for your team to re-engage?
What's the single biggest friction point that caused adoption to drop, and is that something that's fixable on your side, our side, or both?

headline: Orchid Telecom enters the integration roadmap review with an unresolved product escalation from the prior call, a flat health score, and high expansion potential that hinges on roadmap clarity.
objectives: Deliver a concrete update on the integration roadmap timeline and any dependencies, closing out the product escalation from 2026-04-25

T009 is still open with no resolution 13 days after creation; leaving a strategic account's platform team without an answer erodes trust
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Expansion signal is rated high — if the integration roadmap question is resolved favorably, this call is an opportunity to scope which integrations or additional modules would support Orchid Telecom's next rollout and build a joint business case for expansion before the renewal window opens.

When you gave us a 6 on NPS, what was the primary thing driving that score, and what would need to change to move it higher?
As you look toward the rest of 2026, what does a successful partnership with us look like — and how aligned is that picture with what your executive sponsors expect?

headline: Lumen Energy enters this call with persistent permissions confusion and a high ticket volume (8 in 30 days) despite stable usage, creating risk ahead of an August renewal at a health score of 63.
objectives: Complete admin training walkthrough on the permissions model to resolve recurring confusion and reduce ticket volume
Confirm delivery and adoption of the permissions guide promised on the 2026-04-23 call
Surface any broader friction points that may be dragging NPS to 4 ahead of the August renewal

questions_to_ask_customer: Since we last spoke, which specific permissions scenarios are still causing your admins the most confusion or generating the most internal support requests?

headline: Redwood Cloud enters expansion rollout design with strong momentum—active users up 19% in two weeks, health score climbing, and high expansion signal—but a governance plan is still needed before multi-team adoption can scale safely.
objectives: Agree on a governance framework (ownership, permissions, naming conventions) that can support multi-team rollout

questions_to_ask_customer: You've seen active users grow from 244 to 290 in just two weeks—what's driving that organic growth, and which teams are next in line asking for access?

headline: BrightPath Logistics is entering renewal month with a declining health score (61), unresolved high-severity sync failures, and an NPS of 4, making this call critical for stabilization and retention.
